title: feuilleea scalaris
intro: Feuilleea scalaris, also known as Indian ash or ashoka, is a deciduous tree native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Feuilleea scalaris include its ability to support women's health, particularly in menstrual disorders, menopause symptoms, and postpartum care, as well as its use in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Feuilleea scalaris is typically prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or powder, and can be used in various herbal preparations to promote overall well-being.
